response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates exceptional software engineering expertise across multiple domains with 20+ years of experience, the Principal Performance Architect role requires deep specialized knowledge in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design for AI accelerators. His background is entirely in application and systems software development, with no evidence of hardware design, chip architecture, AI accelerator optimization, or deep-learning workload analysis. The gap is fundamental, not learnable in a principal-level role.
strengths: Exceptional breadth of software engineering expertise across web, mobile, embedded systems, and high-performance backends spanning over 20 years
Demonstrated ability to work with complex low-level systems (C++, D-Lang asynchronous I/O, real-time systems) and optimize for performance and latency
Continuous learning across diverse technology stacks suggests capacity to acquire deep technical knowledge in new domains
concerns: No hardware design, SoC modeling, or chip architecture experience despite the role requiring deep expertise in these core areas
AI infrastructure experience consists of software tooling and frameworks, not performance optimization of AI accelerators or deep-learning workload analysis
Entire career is in software development with no evidence of hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, or semiconductor engineering fundamentals

key_factors.factor: AI Accelerator & GPU Hardware Optimization Expertise
